title: Tilt Illusion by Phase

funding_references.funder_name: National Health and Medical Research Council (NHMRC) Australia

description: This dataset contains EEG recordings from 36 participants performing a visual discrimination task involving a tilt illusion paradigm. Participants judged the orientation of a central grating surrounded by an annular grating at ±30 degrees while 64-channel EEG was recorded. The study investigates the prestimulus EEG correlates, specifically alpha oscillation power and phase, that predict variations in tilt illusion magnitude.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using a Biosemi Active2 system with 64 scalp electrodes and 6 EOG electrodes during a visual orientation discrimination task. Stimuli (central target grating surrounded by annular grating at ±30 degrees) were presented for 8.3 ms at 60% contrast. Participants responded using a keyboard. Original data in .bdf format were converted to EEGLab format (.set and .fdt files).
